description.p: Readfence is a clean, modern Markdown viewer built for developers and Linux users. It renders Markdown beautifully by default, with heading hierarchy, code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, links, and blockquotes.

ul.li: Rendered Markdown by default, with a toggleable raw source view
Selectable rendered text you can highlight and copy across headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, and code blocks
Reader-friendly code blocks with language labels and a one-click copy action
A multi-file sidebar for opening and switching between documents
Drag and drop to open Markdown or text files instantly
Automatic reload when an open file changes on disk
Fullscreen reading mode and adjustable font size
A rich theme library including Dark, Light, Dracula, Nord, Gruvbox, Solarized, Tokyo Night, and Catppuccin

screenshot.caption: A Markdown document rendered in Readfence

description.p: A full rendering overhaul, image support, curated themes, and update notifications.

ul.li: Tables render as aligned columns with bold headers
Inline bold, italic, code, and strikethrough styling now renders everywhere
Lists get real bullets, correct nesting, task-list checkboxes, and accent markers
Blockquotes and GitHub-style alerts get accent bars and labels
Local and remote images render in place, including SVG badges
Fifteen curated themes with polished light-mode support; your choice is remembered
Responsive toolbar and refined application chrome
Open files passed on the command line or via a file-manager "Open with" action
Register a desktop entry and icon, and associate Markdown files with Readfence
Initial Flatpak packaging
Reworked the Markdown rendering pipeline
Improved the visual presentation of rendered documents
